channel.title: Microsoft Cloud Platform Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily updates from Azure and Microsoft 365 blogs

item.title: Introducing the Microsoft Sentinel Training Lab. Hands-On Security Operations in Minutes
item.description: A huge thanks to Paul Kew - this lab wouldn't have been possible without his contributions. Security operations is one of those things that’s hard to learn from slides alone. You need to feel what it’s like to triage a multi-stage incident, tune a noisy detection rule, or trace an attacker pivoting...
item.dc:creator: AndreasKapetaniou

item.title: Stop Experimenting, Start Building: AI Apps & Agents Dev Days Has You Covered
item.description: The AI landscape has shifted. The question is no longer “Can we build AI applications?” it’s “Can we build AI applications that actually work in production?” Demos are easy. Reliable, scalable, resilient AI systems that handle real-world complexity? That’s where most teams struggle. If you’re an AI...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: Entra ID Login via Azure Bastion Fails After VM Recreation
item.description: However, you may encounter a confusing scenario where: An Entra ID user attempts to sign in to a Windows VM through Azure Bastion The connection appears to succeed in the backend logs The session is disconnected within a second Bastion returns a generic sign-in error to the user At first glance,...
item.dc:creator: Balajiranganathan

item.title: Write azd hooks in Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, or .NET
item.description: Hooks are one of the most popular features in azd, and now you can write them in Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, or .NET, not just Bash and PowerShell. What’s new? The Azure Developer CLI (azd) hook system now supports four more languages beyond Bash and PowerShell. You can write hook scripts in...
item.dc:creator: Kristen Womack

item.title: Microsoft Sentinel MCP Server with external AI models (Claude) for natural language investigations
item.description: Security teams are increasingly exploring how AI assistants support them in investigating incidents, asking questions, and exploring their data. At the same time, controlling how data is accessed remains critical. Today, we’re sharing how Sentinel can support a third-party AI assistant like Claude...
item.dc:creator: mcasgrain

item.title: 7 tips to optimize Azure Cosmos DB costs for AI and agentic workloads
item.description: AI apps and agentic workloads expose inefficiencies in your data layer faster than any previous generation of apps. You’re storing embeddings, serving low-latency retrieval, handling bursty traffic from chat and orchestration, and often operating across regions. Done right, Azure Cosmos DB can...
item.dc:creator: Michal Toiba

item.title: Public Preview: Azure Arc adds SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machines as a migration target
item.description: Azure Arc migration now supports SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machines as a migration target. You can migrate Arc-enabled SQL Server instances not only to Azure SQL Managed Instance but also to SQL Server running on Azure infrastructure—using the same unified workflow.Learn more.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure Arc adds SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machines as a migration target
item.description: Azure Arc migration now supports SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machines as a migration target. You can migrate Arc-enabled SQL Server instances not only to Azure SQL Managed Instance but also to SQL Server running on Azure infrastructure—using the same unifUpdate Type: Preview, Services: Virtual...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Announcing savings plan for databases: flexible savings for modern, evolving workloads
item.description: As organizations modernize their data platforms, database environments are constantly changing. Teams migrate between services, scale across regions, and evolve architectures to support new applications and AI driven workloads. But optimizing costs in these environments can be challenging,...
item.dc:creator: kyleikeda

item.title: Private subnets by default in Azure Virtual Networks: What changed and how to use NAT Gateway
item.description: Azure is evolving to better support secure‑by‑default cloud architectures. Starting with API version 2025‑07‑01 (released after March 31, 2026), newly created virtual networks now default to using private subnets. This change removes the long‑standing platform behavior of automatically enabling...
item.dc:creator: aimeelittleton

item.title: AI-Powered Downtime Investigation for Azure VMs: Automating Root Cause Analysis
item.description: Co-authors: Jie Su, Abhinav Dua, Mukthar Ahmed, Dhruv Joshi In a previous post, we shared how Azure Automated VM Recovery works to minimize virtual machine downtime through a three-stage approach: Detection, Diagnosis, and Mitigation. This post goes one layer deeper into how our team is using AI to...
item.dc:creator: Jon_Andoni_Baranda

item.title: Optimizing Git policy management at scale
item.description: With just a single improvement in the REST API of Azure DevOps, we achieved a massive reduction in CPU usage and execution time when managing Git policies: 2x less CPU and 10-15x faster execution! This change is already available to all users of Azure DevOps, and it’s time to share a bit more...
item.dc:creator: Azat Galiev

item.title: Public Preview: Logical replication slot sync Status metric for Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server
item.description: You can now monitor the synchronization status of your logical replication slots in Azure Database for PostgreSQL – Flexible Server using the new logical_replication_slot_sync_status metric, now in public preview. This Azure Monitor metric shows whether each logical replication slot is...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Generally Available: Dynamic data masking with Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: Dynamic data masking (DDM) is a server-side, policy-based security feature in Azure Cosmos DB that helps your organization protect sensitive data from unauthorized access. DDM dynamically masks data for nonprivileged users, ensuring that sensitive information is redacted in real time before being...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Generally Available: Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server in Denmark East
item.description: You can now deploy Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server in the Denmark East Azure region. Learn more.
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server in Denmark East
item.description: You can now deploy Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server in the Denmark East Azure region. Learn more.Update Type: GA, Services: Database for PostgreSQL, Categories: Features
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Dynamic data masking with Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: Dynamic data masking (DDM) is a server-side, policy-based security feature in Azure Cosmos DB that helps your organization protect sensitive data from unauthorized access. DDM dynamically masks data for nonprivileged users, ensuring that sensitive informaUpdate Type: GA, Services: Cosmos DB,...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Logical replication slot sync Status metric for Azure PostgreSQL Flexible Server
item.description: You can now monitor the synchronization status of your logical replication slots in Azure Database for PostgreSQL – Flexible Server using the new logical_replication_slot_sync_status metric, now in public preview. This Azure Monitor metric shows whether eUpdate Type: Preview, Services: Database for...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Fast cloud migration, measurable ROI: Forrester Total Economic Impact study of Azure VMware Solution
item.description: Many organizations are balancing near-term continuity for VMware-based workloads with longer-term cloud modernization goals – all while managing cost, security, and resiliency. Azure VMware Solution (AVS) is built for this moment: a Microsoft-managed service verified by VMware that enables running...
item.dc:creator: BritneyCretella

item.title: Introducing Toolboxes in Foundry
item.description: Available in Public Preview Today Toolbox is a new way to curate, configure, and reuse tools across all of your AI agents without rewiring them every time from Foundry. Today, teams build agents across different frameworks and runtimes. Each agent often wires tools directly, with its own...
item.dc:creator: Linda Li, Maria Naggaga, Ronak Chokshi

item.title: Generally Available: Enhanced Mirroring Azure Database for PostgreSQL in Microsoft Fabric
item.description: New capabilities make it easier to mirror real-world Azure Database for PostgreSQL workloads at scale in Microsoft Fabric and operate them with confidence.You can mirror native PostgreSQL data types such as JSON, JSONB and other commonly used types, so richer application schemas flow directly into...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Public Preview: Migrating from virtual network-integrated to Private Endpoint–capable network configuration
item.description: You can now migrate your Azure Database for PostgreSQL server from a virtual network‑integrated deployment to a network configuration that supports Private Endpoint connectivity. If you originally deployed your server inside a virtual network but now want more flexibility in how you manage...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Generally Available: Premium SSD v2 for Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: We are excited to announce the general availability of Premium SSD v2 for Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server. With Premium SSD v2, you can achieve up to four times higher IOPS, significantly lower latency, and better price-performance for I/O-intensive PostgreSQL workloads. With...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Premium SSD v2 for Azure Database for PostgreSQL
item.description: We are excited to announce the general availability of Premium SSD v2 for Azure Database for PostgreSQL flexible server. With Premium SSD v2, you can achieve up to four times higher IOPS, significantly lower latency, and better price-performance for I/O-iUpdate Type: GA, Services: Database for...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Migrating from virtual network-integrated to Private Endpoint–capable network configuration
item.description: You can now migrate your Azure Database for PostgreSQL server from a virtual network‑integrated deployment to a network configuration that supports Private Endpoint connectivity. If you originally deployed your server inside a virtual network but now wantUpdate Type: Preview, Services: Database for...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Enhanced Mirroring Azure Database for PostgreSQL in Microsoft Fabric
item.description: New capabilities make it easier to mirror real-world Azure Database for PostgreSQL workloads at scale in Microsoft Fabric and operate them with confidence.You can mirror native PostgreSQL data types such as JSON, JSONB and other commonly used types, so riUpdate Type: GA, Services: Microsoft Fabric,...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: From Local to Production: The Complete Developer Journey for Building, Composing, and Deploying AI Agents
item.description: When we launched Microsoft Agent Framework last October, we made a promise: building production-grade AI agents should feel as natural and structured as building any other software. Today, we’re delivering on that promise — with the v1.0 release of Microsoft Agent Framework and the general...
item.dc:creator: Takuto Higuchi, jeffhollan

item.title: Introducing the new hosted agents in Foundry Agent Service: secure, scalable compute built for agents
item.description: Agents are already transforming how developers solve problems. Whether it’s a coding agent that refactors your repo overnight, a research agent that synthesizes hundreds of documents into a brief, or an ops agent that monitors and remediates infrastructure — the pattern is clear. Developers are...
item.dc:creator: Takuto Higuchi, jeffhollan, Lakshmi Ramakrishnan

item.title: Azure VNet Data Gateway for Secure Power BI & Power Platform Access in Enterprises
item.description: What Is a VNet data gateway? The VNet data gateway is a Microsoft‑managed gateway service that runs inside a delegated subnet of an Azure Virtual Network. It allows supported Microsoft cloud services—such as Power BI, Power Platform dataflows, and Microsoft Fabric workloads—to securely connect to...
item.dc:creator: kirankumar_manchiwar04

item.title: Understanding the Standard HDD I/O unit size update and what it means for your workloads
item.description: As Azure continues to evolve to support modern workload patterns, we are refining how transactions are measured for Standard HDD Managed Disks. This update helps align billing more closely with actual I/O behavior while maintaining predictable cost controls for customers. In this post, we’ll walk...
item.dc:creator: austin-ma

item.title: Introducing the Microsoft Sentinel Training Lab. Hands-On Security Operations in Minutes
item.description: A huge thanks to Paul Kew - this lab wouldn't have been possible without his contributions. Security operations is one of those things that’s hard to learn from slides alone. You need to feel what ...Update Type: Announcement, Services: Microsoft Sentinel, Categories: Security
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Public Preview: Actual Result for Manual Tests in Azure Test Plans
item.description: We’re excited to announce the public preview of the highly anticipated Actual Result (AR) feature for manual testing in Azure Test Plans! This feature has been one of the top requests of the community, and we’re thrilled to make it available for you. Why use the Actual Result feature? Manual...
item.dc:creator: Panagiotis Liaros

item.title: Stream SQL Change Events to Microsoft Fabric Eventstream with Change Event Streaming for Real-Time Analytics
item.description: Coauthor: Nikola Zagorac In today’s data-driven world, the ability to capture and act on data changes in real time is a competitive necessity. From financial services processing millions of transactions per second to e-commerce platforms tracking inventory and customer behavior, real-time data...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Outbound access protection for Data Factory
item.description: Co-author: Abhishek Narain Workspace outbound access protection (OAP) is widely accessible for Data Factory workloads—including Pipelines, Copy Job, and Dataflows—as well as for Mirrored Databases such as Mirrored SQL Database and Mirrored Snowflake. Key benefits Enhanced outbound security: By...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Customer Managed Keys (CMK) for Eventhouse
item.description: Empowering customers with enhanced security and control. This milestone marks a significant step forward in empowering our customers to take control of their data security and compliance needs within the Eventhouse platform. With CMK, you can manage your own encryption keys, providing an added...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: If You're Building AI on Azure, ECS 2026 is Where You Need to Be
item.description: Let me be direct: there's a lot of noise in the conference calendar. Generic cloud events. Vendor showcases dressed up as technical content. Sessions that look great on paper but leave you with nothing you can actually ship on Monday. ECS 2026 isn't that. As someone who will be on stage at Cologne...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: NFS Permission Denied in Azure App Service on Linux: What It Means and What to Do
item.description: If your Azure App Service on Linux uses an Azure Files NFS share, you may sometimes see errors like Permission denied or Errno 13 when your app tries to write to the mounted path. Azure Files supports NFS for Linux and Unix workloads, and NFS uses Unix-style numeric ownership and permissions...
item.dc:creator: michelleyau

item.title: General Availability Refresh: Mirroring Azure Database for PostgreSQL in Microsoft Fabric
item.description: Introduction We are excited to announce the General Availability Refresh of Mirroring Azure Database for PostgreSQL in Microsoft Fabric. This update introduces several new capabilities designed to ...Update Type: GA, Services: Microsoft Fabric, Database for PostgreSQL, Categories:
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Getting Started with the SUSE Multi-Linux Manager MCP Server and GitHub Copilot
item.description: Enterprise Linux environments are heterogeneous. That's not a problem statement - it's just the truth. SUSE, Ubuntu, RHEL, and their downstream variants coexist in every data center I've seen, and increasingly across Azure subscriptions too. AI assistants like GitHub Copilot can already connect to...
item.dc:creator: abbottkarl

item.title: Azure SDK Release (April 2026)
item.description: Azure SDK releases every month. In this post, you'll find this month's highlights and release notes. The post Azure SDK Release (April 2026) appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Ronnie Geraghty

item.title: Ingest at Scale, Securely — Azure Monitor pipeline Is Now Generally Available
item.description: Today, we're thrilled to announce the general availability of Azure Monitor pipeline — a telemetry pipeline built for secure, high-scale ingestion across any environment. But the best way to understand what makes it powerful isn't to start with features. It's to start with the problems that kept...
item.dc:creator: XemaPathak

item.title: Generally Available: Azure Monitor pipeline
item.description: Azure Monitor pipeline is now generally available.Azure Monitor pipeline provides a centralized control point for telemetry ingestion and transformation, designed specifically for secure, high‑throughput, enterprise‑scale scenarios. It's built on open-source technologies from the OpenTelemetry...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure Monitor pipeline
item.description: Azure Monitor pipeline is now generally available.Azure Monitor pipeline provides a centralized control point for telemetry ingestion and transformation, designed specifically for secure, high‑throughput, enterprise‑scale scenarios. It's built on open-souUpdate Type: GA, Services: Azure Monitor,...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: The Polyglot tax – Part 4
item.description: The Agent-Ready Database: Security, Backup, and MCP Part 4 of 4 – The Multi-Model Database Series This is the final post in a four-part series on multi-model databases in SQL Server 2025 and Azure SQL – exploring how the optimizer, storage engine, and security layer treat each data model as a...
item.dc:creator: Aditya Badramraju

item.title: Generally Available: Azure NetApp Files advanced ransomware protection
item.description: Azure NetApp Files advanced ransomware protection (ANF ARP) is now in GA (General Availability). ANF ARP is designed to help organizations proactively detect, respond to, and recover from ransomware threats on cloud volumes. ANF ARP monitors Azure NetApp Files volumes for suspicious activity using...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Transition to Entra ID (formerly known as Azure AD) to query data from Azure Monitor application insights by September 30, 2026
item.description: *Note: Previously announced to be retired March 31, 2026. We have extended the retirement date to September 30, 2026. On September 30, 2026, API keys used query application insights will be retired. ou'll need to transition to Microsoft Entra ID (formerl [Sevice: Microsoft Entra ID, Target Date: 01...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure NetApp Files advanced ransomware protection
item.description: Azure NetApp Files advanced ransomware protection (ANF ARP) is now in GA (General Availability). ANF ARP is designed to help organizations proactively detect, respond to, and recover from ransomware threats on cloud volumes. ANF ARP monitors Azure NetAppUpdate Type: GA, Services: Azure NetApp...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: I Wasted 68 Minutes a Day Re-Explaining My Code. Then I Built auto-memory.
item.description: ~1,900 lines of Python. Zero dependencies. Saves you an hour a day. GitHub → · pip install auto-memory Now give Copilot CLI enhanced context recall. Point it at deploy/install.md and let it cook. 🍳 Are you tired of using the slash /compact command every 10 min? The Context Window Is a Lie Every AI...
item.dc:creator: Desi Villanueva

item.title: General Availability: Dynamic Data Masking for Azure Cosmos DB
item.description: Protecting sensitive data is a foundational requirement for modern applications. As organizations scale their use of Azure Cosmos DB across teams, environments, and workloads, controlling who can see sensitive information becomes increasingly important. Today, we are excited to announce the general...
item.dc:creator: Sudhanshu Khera

item.title: Aspire 13.2: Bun Support and Container Enhancements
item.description: Aspire 13.2 keeps the JavaScript story moving with Bun for Vite, more predictable containers, and small but meaningful fixes across debugging and builds. The post Aspire 13.2: Bun Support and Container Enhancements appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: David Pine
